calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2026 Off Calendar

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to Open Court Mr. Tittman informs the clerk there is a dismissal with prejudice filed and nothing needs to be done by the court. The clerk reports to chambers. At the instruction of the court, the clerk informs counsel the matter will not be heard and the tentative ruling will be adopted.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AT-BAY INSURANCE SERVICES, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Defendant At-Bay Insurance Services, LLC's unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Matthew Bricker Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Matthew Bricker has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) The requirements are quite straightforward and set out clearly in the Rule of Court. Counsel should take care to fulfill them.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
